title: Chitrapathi connected to Madhavi household
claim_or_observation: Chitrapathi is identified as Madhavi's mother and belongs to the artistic household context

title: Chitrapathi notices festival absence
claim_or_observation: She notices Madhavi and Manimekalai do not attend Indra festival and sends Vasanthamālai
notes: Establish reputation and household continuity function

title: Performing women possess extensive training
claim_or_observation: The epic describes arts and skills associated with women performers including music dance and related knowledge
notes: Avoid reducing performance to sensual display

title: Chitrapathi approaches Udayakumaran
claim_or_observation: Chitrapathi goes to Udayakumaran after hearing of Manimekalai's renunciation

title: Chitrapathi tells stories before the prince
claim_or_observation: She uses narratives and social reasoning while speaking with Udayakumaran

title: Chitrapathi seeks queen's help
claim_or_observation: She goes to the queen and asks for Manimekalai's release

title: Chitrapathi preserves household memory
claim_or_observation: Her actions are connected to lineage reputation and survival of an artistic family

title: Security through patronage worldview
claim_or_observation: Chitrapathi interprets elite relationships as protection in a vulnerable social environment
notes: Do not present as historical biography

title: Protection becomes possession
claim_or_observation: Her survival strategy becomes harmful when imposed on Manimekalai's autonomous vocation

title: Not a generic villain
claim_or_observation: Chitrapathi should not be reduced to jealous elder stereotype

title: Not all performing women share one identity
claim_or_observation: Her household experience cannot define all ancient Tamil performers

title: Udayakumaran retains agency
claim_or_observation: Her influence is one factor in the prince's pursuit but his choices remain his responsibility

title: Queen plea as accountability
claim_or_observation: Her humiliation before the queen demonstrates consequences and responsibility

title: Generational conflict
claim_or_observation: Chitrapathi and Madhavi represent different responses to the same social pressures

title: Ornament is not moral judgement
claim_or_observation: Costume and wealth cannot visually label Chitrapathi as corrupt

title: Freedom as another survival strategy
claim_or_observation: Chitrapathi's final growth is recognition that Manimekalai's path can also protect life
